questions.question: What does the Vue compiler do when it encounters `await` in `<script setup>`?
options: It throws a compile-time error requiring Suspense to be added first
It wraps the setup block in an `async setup()` function automatically
It defers execution to the `onMounted` hook
It converts the `await` into a `watchEffect` call

questions.question: In plain Vue (without Nuxt), what happens if a component uses top-level `await` but has no `<Suspense>` ancestor?
options: The component throws a runtime error and the page crashes
The component renders with empty data and logs a console warning
The component stays in a pending state and never renders
Vue falls back to synchronous rendering and ignores the await

questions.question: Why should you prefer `useFetch` over raw `await fetch()` in Nuxt pages?
options: Raw `fetch` does not support async/await syntax in Nuxt
`useFetch` integrates with Nuxt's payload system and avoids double-fetching on hydration
`useFetch` automatically adds authentication headers from the session
Raw `fetch` does not work inside `<script setup>`
